---
title: "Hands-On Demo: Software Containers"
subtitle: "Building and running Docker containers for reproducible research"
author:
  - name: "Fabio Molo"
    orcid: 0000-0001-9320-5854
format:
  html:
    toc-depth: 4
execute:
  eval: false
---

This hands-on demo requires Docker Desktop (available at <https://www.docker.com/products/docker-desktop/>). Visit the [instructions](../../instructions/index.qmd) page for setup details.

# Getting started with Docker

<!-- TODO: add introductory exercises here -->

## Exercise 1: Pulling and running an existing image

<!-- TODO -->

## Exercise 2: Writing a Dockerfile

<!-- TODO -->

## Exercise 3: Building and running your own image

<!-- TODO -->

# Containers for R research

## Exercise 4: Using a Rocker image

<!-- TODO -->

## Exercise 5: Installing packages inside a container

<!-- TODO -->

## Exercise 6: Mounting your project directory

<!-- TODO -->

# Putting it all together

## Exercise 7: Combining Git, Make, and Docker

<!-- TODO -->

# Additional Resources

-   Docker documentation: <https://docs.docker.com/get-started/>
-   Rocker project: <https://rocker-project.org/>
-   CRS Primer on Software Containers: <https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.13757230>
-   The Turing Way — Reproducible Environments: <https://the-turing-way.netlify.app/reproducible-research/renv>
